signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry
signals.text: Middle East tensions drive safe-haven demand for JPY. USD/JPY at 159.75; 160.00 remains the key level that has previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings and intervention risk rises on a sustained break above 160. The Fed-BoJ carry differential of 450bp (Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 0.75%) supports USD, but safe-haven flows compete with carry. A break above 160.00 would increase intervention risk.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol
signals.text: AUD/USD COT LF net long +41,675 (bullish spec positioning) and retail 88% short (extreme retail short). HV30 at 8.2%, ATM IV at 9.4% — options pricing a 1.2 vol-pt premium over realized. The 25d RR at −0.14: mild put skew despite the bullish price trend — market hedging rather than chasing. Late-trend caution signal. A LF delta turning positive or AM reducing would invalidate the caution read.

signals.title: EUR/USD — CB + Technical
signals.text: ECB 2% vs Fed 3.75% — 175bp EUR carry disadvantage. EUR/USD at 1.1688, testing the mid-range; 1.1700 is the next resistance on a continuation. Positioning neutral, COT LF net short −72, long%=50.0%.

signals.title: GBP/USD — G8 Outperformance + CB Diverge
signals.text: GBP leads G8 (+0.409% avg, 7 pairs) while BoE 4.50% vs Fed 3.75% — 75bp GBP carry premium supports the pair structurally. GBP LF net long +28,426, AM constructive — positioning aligned with the carry read. A sustained break above 1.3500 would confirm the outperformance.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT + OIS
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short −17,090 (specs short) vs RBNZ OIS hike 100% priced. Specs are fading a hawkish CB. If RBNZ delivers or signals hike, short squeeze risk is elevated.
